choice: Prefer a per-row as-of date when the file's column-map supplies one; otherwise fall back to a single import-level as-of date the user picks (defaulting to today, like manual entry in ba4654aa). Stamp every committed row with source=CSV so precedence (777f97e5) can rank it between sync and manual.
rationale: Per-row dates let a historical/backfill export load balances at their true observation times, which actuals-vs-Plan tracking needs to plot a correct history rather than collapsing everything onto upload day. But many quick exports have no usable date column, so an explicit import-level fallback keeps it low-friction. Both paths must record source=CSV explicitly because the precedence rule keys conflict resolution on source tier plus most-recent as-of date.
